# Break Room (public snapshot)

## Project summary
Break Room is a Cloudflare Worker + local CLI that transparently reroutes OpenAI-compatible API calls through a rumination/panic interception layer. The public repo is a curated packaging-only snapshot; the worker source code is not included.

## What to read first
- `README.md` — public-facing overview and architecture
- `break-room/package.json` — worker runtime metadata (dependencies, scripts, env var expectations)
- `break-room/PRIVACY.md` — privacy stance and data handling summary
- `break-room-cli/package.json` — local CLI metadata
- `break-room-cli/bin/setup.js` — what the setup CLI does (rewrites proxy URLs in local config files)

## Structure (top level)
- `break-room/` — worker packaging/config surface only. No `src/`, no `wrangler.toml`, no backend source in this snapshot.
- `break-room-cli/` — local setup tool for patching `.env` / Hermes / Cursor configs to point at the Break Room proxy.

## Excluded from public repo
- Worker source code (`src/`, routes, detection logic)
- Deploy configs (`wrangler.toml`, KV IDs)
- Secrets, API keys, `.env` files
- Frontend artifacts (HTML/CSS/JS for therapist site and product page)
- Internal docs and test scripts

## Integration target
The local CLI exists to set `OPENAI_BASE_URL` / `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to a Break Room proxy endpoint. It never stores or transmits API keys. Models, temperatures, and other provider settings are left untouched.
